label: Azure Cosmos DB
description: Connect to a Microsoft Azure Cosmos DB account to query and manage globally-distributed, multi-model data.

form.title: Connect Azure Cosmos DB
form.subtitle: Paste your Cosmos DB account endpoint and primary key from the Azure Portal to get started.

methods.label: Account Endpoint + Primary Key
methods.description: Paste your Cosmos DB account endpoint URI and primary key directly from the Azure Portal. The fastest way to connect — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with Account Endpoint and Primary Key

This is the simplest and most direct way to authenticate with Azure Cosmos DB.

### Steps

1. Open the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com) and navigate to your **Cosmos DB account**.
2. In the left sidebar, click **Settings → Keys**.
3. Copy the **URI** — this is your account endpoint (e.g. `https://your-account.documents.azure.com:443/`).
4. Copy the **PRIMARY KEY** (a long base-64 string). Avoid using the read-only key unless you only need read access.
5. Paste both values into the fields below.
6. Optionally specify a **Database ID** and **Container ID** to scope Anton to a specific dataset; leave blank to connect at the account level.

### Notes

- The primary key grants full data-plane access to all databases in the account. If you need read-only access, use the **PRIMARY READ-ONLY KEY** from the same page.
- Keys can be regenerated in the Azure Portal under **Settings → Keys → Regenerate Primary Key** — if you do, re-paste the new key here.
- If your Cosmos DB account uses a **virtual network** or **IP firewall**, make sure the machine running Anton is in the allowed range.
- For production use, consider creating a **role assignment** via Azure RBAC and using the service principal method instead.

fields.name: account_endpoint
fields.label: Account Endpoint URI
fields.description: Found in Azure Portal → Cosmos DB account → Settings → Keys → URI.

fields.name: primary_key
fields.label: Primary Key
fields.description: Found in Azure Portal → Cosmos DB account → Settings → Keys → PRIMARY KEY. Use the read-only key if you only need read access.

fields.name: database_id
fields.label: Database ID
fields.description: Optional. The name of the specific Cosmos DB database to connect to. Leave blank to connect at the account level.

fields.name: container_id
fields.label: Container ID
fields.description: Optional. The name of the specific container (collection) within the database. Requires Database ID to be set.

methods.label: Azure Service Principal (RBAC)
methods.description: Authenticate using an Azure Active Directory service principal with Cosmos DB RBAC role assignments. Better for team or production environments where key rotation is undesirable.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with an Azure Service Principal

Use this method to authenticate via Azure Active Directory without sharing raw account keys.

### Prerequisites

- You need an Azure AD **App Registration** (service principal) with a client secret.
- The service principal must be assigned a Cosmos DB **data-plane role** (e.g. `Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor`).

### Steps

1. In the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com), open **Azure Active Directory → App registrations → New registration**.
2. Give it a name (e.g. `anton-cosmosdb`), then click **Register**.
3. Note the **Application (client) ID** and **Directory (tenant) ID** from the Overview page.
4. Go to **Certificates & secrets → New client secret**, set an expiry, and copy the **Value** immediately — it won't be shown again.
5. In your Cosmos DB account, open **Access control (IAM)** or use the **Data Explorer → Manage role assignments** to assign the service principal the `Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor` (or `Reader`) role.
6. Paste the tenant ID, client ID, client secret, and account endpoint into the fields below.

### Notes

- The client secret expires — check the expiry date you set and rotate before it lapses.
- For tighter scope, assign only the minimum role needed (e.g. read-only).
- RBAC data-plane roles are distinct from Azure resource-level IAM roles — make sure you assign the Cosmos DB-specific data role, not just a subscription-level role.

fields.name: account_endpoint
fields.label: Account Endpoint URI
fields.description: Found in Azure Portal → Cosmos DB account → Settings → Keys → URI.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.label: Tenant ID
fields.description: Your Azure Active Directory tenant ID, found in AAD → Overview → Directory (tenant) ID.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID (Application ID)
fields.description: The Application (client) ID of your AAD App Registration.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: The client secret value from AAD → App registrations → Certificates & secrets. Shown only once at creation.

fields.name: database_id
fields.label: Database ID
fields.description: Optional. The name of the specific Cosmos DB database to connect to.

fields.name: container_id
fields.label: Container ID
fields.description: Optional. The name of the specific container within the database. Requires Database ID to be set.
